reviews.review_msg: Peter Tingling was rude and dismissive at SFU, only paying attention to those who did case comps, etc. Despite my involvement, I avoided him due to his personality. Even post-graduation, he remained rude. Why choose to be miserable to 20-year-olds?

What happens when you cover short man syndrome and childhood neglect with a layer of arrogance? You get Peter tingling. Peter's anecdotes, which he spends most of his lectures on, mainly include his wife and his "superiority." He is the part of BBA that everyone wants to avoid. Take his course to learn about the bad people of the business world.
good prof
Very intelligent and approachable guy, would definitely recommend him. Fair grader and you'll learn a lot.

Far too full of himself. Tingling is one of those people who is very knowledgeable but has very little personable qualities. His lectures are hard and off Topic. He is rude and unfair. Condescending is a word that springs to mind. If you want to do well, do NOT take the course with him, otherwise be prepared for the most work you have ever done.

Avoid him if you can. He makes a business foundation course harder than it should be. He marks all the assignments and exams by himself so look out for low grades. Boring lectures, very intimidating and he doesn't even respect his audience enough to put on a mic. Overall, not a good prof choice for BUS201
Class is fair, just study and you'll be fine

Avoid him if you can! This professor is full of himself and often spend the majority of class time talking about irrelevant things. He makes the exam extremely hard which is funny because when in class, everything he talks about sounds simple and he gives very little explanation on the technical parts.
Really smart guy, really passionate about the material. He does pick students at random to answer his questions, but you get used to that.
Easy class, read the text book and you'll do great but dont underestimate the final like i did

He picks random people in lectures to answer questions, most of them are opinion based,  but i'd rather he open up a discussion where people can express their opinions if they choose to.  He tries to make the class interesting but I literally have to force myself to attend his lectures. He is not the best prof for 237.

I really like him as a prof, I think you could do a lot worse than him. He expects a lot from his students, and tests are hard, but those marks can easily be made up for in assignments. He's very concise, will sometimes say a sentence in class not on the slides that will appear on an exam, so skipping class is not an option.

I took Bus 201 and Bus 237 with Peter.  Lectures were dull at points and not necessary to attend but he does do a good job of relating course material to current events.  Exams are straightforward for the most part but he marks written answers himself and is a tough grader.

He gives you all the material you need to know in the lecture slides.  The exams are 60 multiple choice & 4 short answer questions. The final is cumulative and it is difficult. The assignments are alright - the grading depends on your TA.
tough graded but good pro
really arrogant, lectures are long and off topic.  Usually ends up talking about himself, HBR articles are long and pointless
Avoid Peter if you can, you will prob fail his class without sufficient readings. The assignments take up a lot of time but worth very little.
Really good prof. Took 201 a required course and he was great. Intimidating in class but very approachable in his office hours. Fair exams. Really good prof.

A very intimidating prof. His lectures were fairly interesting, but all you mainly need to pass the course is the textbook. He marks quite difficulty, and you never seem to know his expectations until after you've done poorly on something. In that regards he is not a very clear teacher. Although he is knowledgeable, would not take his courses again
Not sure he is talking about on the lecture all the time, sound related to the topic but actually not. Rude, Rude, Rude!!! If possible, do not take his courses.

Super tough Tests, the rest of the content is very easy including the weekly quizzes. the quizzes are word for word from the book but the tests have very tricky multiple choice. It's definitely one of those "choose the best answer" type.

Did not like him as an intro to business course, very intimidating and tough. Encourages discussion but shuts down people. Important lecture, kinda interesting, but not very relevant to the textbook.  Exams are very much related to the textbook and they were between fair and hard.  If you can, take the course with another prof!!!

Good guy all around, you can tell that he genuinely likes teaching. Good use of "news", keeps material current. Textbook is mandatory, you could probably get away with a later edition, but you definitely need it for the online quizzes and in-class assignments. Midterm and Final are formatted the same - mc is easy, written is marked hard.

Love him! If you can get through him, you can get through any prof. Had him for a mandatory first year Bus course, everyone had their opinions. He's up front, it's upto you whether you can handle it or not. A lot of student engagement (as you would expect in a business class). Show up, participate and know your key terms. That's all!
Your grade is dependent on your group projects. Pass the exams with flying colours but get stuck with a bad group usually results in a ok grade

Lectures was completely irrelevant from the textbook since he just talks about life. But the exams were mainly on the textbook. Remember his favorite HBR's and you will be good to go. He's not that helpful or clear on assignments. Even the TA's don't know what he wants sometimes. His class is a lot of self learning & guessing what he wants from you

Though he might sound tough at first, he really really wants to see his students succeed. His lectures are boring if you only half pay attention to them. Fully immerse yourself into his lecture and you'll find that they stuff he talks about not only helps with exam prep but applies to all aspects of life.

Peter was the only professor that was informative and engaging in his lectures. I was never bored for a second and I felt like I was actually prepared for university. I can't wait to take bus 237 with him. Would recommend.
He's not bad; we're just dumb.
Good guy.

The lectures are really essential for passing. He basically gives you the material you need to know in class. Textbook is like the lecture. He makes you read it before class. It is good to read before because then you have a basic understanding before attending lecture.

1 1/2 years after this course, I know that no other professor could've prepared me for university like he did. He expects a lot, but helps you achieve ambitious goals. There are 2 types of tasks: Important and urgent ones. Getting to know professors is an important one. Take initiative, visit office hours, be driven and you won't be disappointed.

hard exams, lectures quite boring and he frequently goes off topic. I found the assignments interesting, and it is important to do well on them if you want a good grade. Overall, not a prof I would recommend

I had a horrible time in this class. He changed due dates last minute often and he had us convinced it was all for the sake of fairness. All marks are final or fairness would have been violated. His lectures are disparate from his tests resulting in borderline failing averages and he releases no extra study materials.

OK, he tried very hard to be funny, but he is cold when you ask him some question!! he is hot in during the lecture, but after lecture, he is super cold! he do not want to answer any question, it seems asking him any question is wasting his time!!!
